title: DoorDash launches Ask DoorDash for prompt- and photo-based ordering
friendlyTitle: DoorDash adds an AI assistant for food and grocery orders
summary: DoorDash is rolling out Ask DoorDash, an AI chatbot that lets users search for food, build grocery carts from recipes or photos, reorder items, and find reservations using natural-language requests. The feature is launching on iOS in select regions before broader U.S. expansion.
friendlySummary: Ask DoorDash lets people describe what they want, use photos or recipes to build grocery carts, reorder items, and look for reservations. It is starting on iOS in selected regions.
whyItMatters: This is AI in a very ordinary workflow: deciding what to eat, translating a recipe into groceries, and narrowing choices without scrolling. It shows consumer AI moving into task completion rather than just answering questions.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could save time on a common daily chore: deciding what to buy or eat. It is a good example of AI becoming a practical assistant inside an app people already use.
